caption:
Looking for things to do in Florida? 👀 This spot (plus 1,000+ more) is in my Florida Travel Guide + Interactive Map perfect for weekend getaways, road trips, and exploring without hours of research and stress! 💬 Comment “GUIDE” and I’ll send it to you!

Most people drive right past it on the way to Siesta Key or Sarasota, but Venice is slower, quieter, and way more laid-back — with great beaches, a quaint downtown, and some of the best sunsets on the Gulf.

It’s known as the “Shark Tooth Capital of the World” because prehistoric shark teeth constantly wash up on the beaches here. People come from all over just to hunt for them, especially at Venice Beach and Caspersen Beach, and it’s actually one of the easiest places in the country to find them.

A little history: Venice was developed in the 1920s and modeled after Venice, Italy — which is why you’ll see Mediterranean-style buildings around town. 

What to do when you visit:
• Shark tooth hunt at Venice or Caspersen Beach
• Walk or bike the Venetian Waterway Park & Legacy Trail
• Explore downtown Venice Ave for shops, coffee, and restaurants
• Catch a sunset on the Venice Fishing Pier
• Grab seafood or a drink by the water at Sharky’s or Fin’s

If you love low-key beach towns with real character, Venice should be on your Florida list. 🌊
